scope: Local RealVisXL one-hand visible table-contact fallback after two-hand separation repeatedly failed prompt compliance.

improvement_observed: Portrait composition is centered and stable with full face inside the frame, both eyes visible, coherent hair, wardrobe, lighting, and background.
Hands are in the lower frame with plausible table contact, believable skin texture, clear wrist accessories, and usable contact shadows.
The image avoids the severe face crop seen in the object-contact robustness sample.
remaining_notes: The fallback prompt requested one visible right hand with the left hand outside frame, but the output includes two overlapping hands.
Finger/contact detail is acceptable for local iteration, but prompt compliance is still incomplete for a one-hand certification target.
This supports using simpler contact targets but does not yet certify RealVisXL hand/contact robustness.

defects.message: The output shows two overlapping hands instead of one isolated visible right hand.

qa_decision.reason: The sample is a good local portrait/contact iteration, but it did not obey the one-hand-only target and therefore cannot certify hand/contact prompt robustness.
qa_decision.recommended_next_action: Either accept RealVisXL hands/contact as local pass-with-notes only, or move to a different local-first lane where certification criteria are not blocked by repeated hand-pose prompt drift.
